items.title: CMS guidance: federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds limited for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued a State Health Official letter to help states implement a statutory change that limits when states can claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or U.S. nationals, or who fall into certain noncitizen categories in statute, starting October 1, 2026. CMS emphasized that federal match remains available for emergency medical conditions and said the change does not affect states’ option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant women in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI benefits increase 2.8% in 2026 (COLA)
items.summary: SSA says Social Security and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefits for about 75 million people will increase 2.8% in 2026. SSA says the increase begins with benefits payable in January 2026 for Social Security beneficiaries, and increased SSI payments begin December 31, 2025.

items.title: Public comment deadline Apr. 27, 2026 for HUD proposed rule changing eviction notice requirements in HUD-assisted housing
items.summary: A state bulletin summarizing HUD’s proposal says HUD is considering removing a requirement that PHAs and housing providers give tenants 30 days’ notice before ending a lease for unpaid rent and 30 days to pay past-due rent. The bulletin says the public comment deadline is Monday, April 27, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET.

items.title: Federal Register: HUD reopened public comments until May 6, 2026 on SFMIOS privacy notice modification (Do Not Pay routine use)
items.summary: HUD published a Federal Register notice reopening the public comment period until May 6, 2026 on a second modification to the SFMIOS system-of-records notice, after HUD identified an error in the previously published modification. The notice discusses replacing a routine use related to Treasury’s Do Not Pay system for reviewing payment and award eligibility to help identify, prevent, or recover improper payments.

items.title: USDA WIC memo: states must add at least one non-fresh fruit/vegetable option by April 2026
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 says state agencies must authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026. The memo is part of recent WIC food package updates and is intended to expand participants’ options.

items.title: HUD Exchange: HOTMA Final Rule compliance date extended to Jan. 1, 2026 for CPD programs using Part 5 income regs
items.summary: HUD says it extended the compliance date of the HOTMA Final Rule to January 1, 2026 for all Community Planning and Development (CPD) programs that use HUD’s 24 CFR Part 5 income regulations. HUD also noted grantees that are ready may choose an earlier compliance date within the allowed window.

items.title: IRS reminder: request a filing extension by April 15, 2026 to file by Oct. 15
items.summary: The IRS reminded taxpayers they can file Form 4868 by April 15, 2026 to get an automatic extension to file their federal return until Oct. 15, 2026. The IRS also emphasized that any tax owed is still due April 15 to avoid interest and possible penalties, even if you extend your filing date.

items.title: Education Department: 10M+ 2026–27 FAFSA forms completed; 17% higher than last cycle
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education said more than 10 million 2026–27 FAFSA forms have been completed and processed, which it described as a 17% increase versus the same point in the prior cycle. The Department also said updated College Scorecard earnings data began powering the FAFSA earnings indicator starting March 22, 2026.

items.title: Federal Student Aid: 2025–26 FAFSA must be received by June 30, 2026 for federal aid
items.summary: The 2025–26 FAFSA PDF says the federal submission deadline is June 30, 2026 (applications can be submitted as early as Oct. 1, 2024). It also notes state and college deadlines can be earlier, so students should submit as early as possible.

items.title: HUD: 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding guidance and shortfall policies expected in spring 2026
items.summary: HUD’s 2026 Budget Management Letter says PHAs should receive their 2026 HAP funding amounts in spring 2026 after HUD completes reconciliation of 2025 data, and that final 2026 shortfall policies will be released with the 2026 HCV funding implementation notice. HUD also estimates a 99% HAP proration factor and notes that 2025 shortfall PHAs remain under restrictions unless released after 2026 allocations.

items.title: KFF: 2025 reconciliation law Medicaid changes projected to raise uninsured by 7.5M by 2034
items.summary: KFF reports Medicaid policy changes enacted in the 2025 reconciliation law are estimated to increase the number of people without health insurance by 7.5 million in 2034, with 5.3 million of those newly uninsured tied to new Medicaid work requirements. KFF notes the law also pauses parts of prior eligibility/enrollment rules, restricts eligibility for some lawfully present immigrants, and requires more frequent redeterminations for ACA expansion adults.

items.title: USDA SNAP: guidance pending on 2025 law changes to ABAWD exception/waiver criteria
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP work requirements page says it is still developing guidance on how the “One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025” changes ABAWD work requirements, including changes to ABAWD exception criteria and ABAWD waiver criteria. The page reiterates that ABAWDs can keep benefits beyond the time limit by meeting an 80-hours-per-month work or qualifying activity requirement.
